---
title: "Deploying RAG in Bedrock vs. Local: WattBot 2025 Case Study"
date: 2026-02-17
author:
  - "Nils Matteson"
  - "Blaise Enuh"
  - "Chris Endemann"
image: "https://img.youtube.com/vi/WYSzI3WZmKo/maxresdefault.jpg"

language:
  title-block-author-single: "Presenter"
  title-block-author-plural: "Presenters"
  title-block-published: "Date"

categories:
  - Videos
  - ML+X
  - UW-Madison
  - RAG
  - Retrieval
  - LLM
  - Cloud
  - AWS
  - Bedrock
  - Hugging Face
  - Foundation models
  - GenAI
  - Sustainability
  - Energy
  - Benchmarking
  - GPU
  - Deep learning
  - NLP
---

## About this resource
Many researchers are exploring retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to build document-grounded, trustworthy AI tools, but it is often unclear how design choices around models, infrastructure, and deployment play out in practice. In this session, we present lessons learned from replicating the winning RAG system from the WattBot 2025 challenge. This challenge focused on producing citation-backed energy and sustainability estimates for AI workloads from a fixed corpus of 30+ academic papers, or explicitly abstaining when evidence was missing. After a short overview of the winning approach, presenters discuss how this system was implemented in practice, including a cloud deployment using AWS Bedrock and local, open-source efforts (e.g., Hugging Face models on GB10 or PowerEdge 7725). We share lessons learned around performance, cost, latency, and operational tradeoffs, along with a demo of a Streamlit-based interface designed for longer-term hosting—aimed at making RAG tools accessible to stakeholders.

#### Deploying RAG in Bedrock vs. Local — Nils Matteson & Blaise Enuh
Nils Matteson (CS/DS Undergraduate, UW-Madison) and Blaise Enuh (Research Associate, Great Lakes Bioenergy Research Center, UW-Madison) walk through both a cloud-based deployment using AWS Bedrock and a local, open-source alternative using Hugging Face models on NVIDIA GB10 and Dell PowerEdge 7725 hardware. They compare performance, cost, latency, and operational tradeoffs between the two approaches.

- **Nils's website**: [nilsmatteson.com](https://nilsmatteson.com/)

#### WattBot Challenge Overview & Streamlit Demo — Chris Endemann
Chris Endemann (Research Cloud Consultant, DoIT, UW-Madison) provides a brief overview of the winning WattBot 2025 approach and demos a Streamlit-based interface designed for longer-term hosting.

{{< video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WYSzI3WZmKo >}}

### Links
- **GitHub**: [KohakuRAG](https://github.com/matteso1/KohakuRAG)
- **Kaggle challenge**: [WattBot 2025](https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/WattBot2025/overview)
- **Winning solution**: [KohakuBlueleaf/KohakuRAG](https://github.com/KohakuBlueleaf/KohakuRAG)
- [Machine Learning Marathon](https://ml-marathon.wisc.edu/): Learn about the annual Machine Learning Marathon (3-month AI/ML hackathon) hosted by ML+X each fall.

## See also
- [**Talk**: AI's Environmental Footprint: Insights and Actions](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Applications/Videos/Forums/mlx_2025-09-09.html): The previous ML+X forum where WattBot was first introduced, covering AI sustainability measurement and the RAG-based challenge design.
- [**Notebook**: Exploring Fact-Based QA with RAG: Romeo and Juliet](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Learn/Notebooks/2025-05-07_RAG-Romeo-Juliet.html): Learn how to build an end-to-end RAG pipeline from scratch using sentence-transformers and Hugging Face models.
- [**Workshop**: Intro to AWS SageMaker for Predictive ML/AI](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Learn/Workshops/Intro-Amazon_SageMaker.html): Hands-on workshop covering AWS SageMaker and Bedrock for cloud-based ML/AI workflows, including RAG deployment.
- [**Resource**: UW Generative AI Services & Policies](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/GenAI/GenAI-at-UW-Madison.html): Overview of vetted GenAI tools at UW-Madison, including AWS Bedrock access.
